Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Joke card represents a funny situation. It may be as simple as a pun, or as complicated as a wildly complex sequence of rational, normal seeming events that leads to an ultimately absurd situation.

Significator.Plot: The Joke Plot is traditionally known as a Comedy. Comedies are usually built around a central misunderstanding, or confusion that creates the comedic situation. Classically these are cases of mistaken identity, suspected infidelity, misunderstandings, gossip and rumour. In more modern comedies, these components may be present along with satire, parody, witty banter, comedic insults, social awkwardness, and slapstick to create funny, farcical, or just cringe-worthy situations.
Significator.Side: A Side with The Joke Significator is usually comedic relief, and is often considered a joke by the other sides. In some genres, and under some Yarn-Tellers the Joke Side can always invoke the "Rule of Funny" to get by, survive or even succeed, depending upon how funny the Joke is...
Significator.Character:
The Joke Significator indicates a Character who is a comedian.

They Gain Yin when they act stupid or uncomprehending.

They Gain Yang when they tell jokes, or attempt humour.

They Gain Chi when anyone laughs.

They also gain +1 Success Levels on any Positive Psyche Attacks that they may attempt.
Significator.Scene: The Joke Scene is intended for a moment of comic relief, a reduction of tension with a silly situation. Although occasionally a Prank can heighten tensions rather than relieve them. Yarn-Tellers can play the card to create Pranks and Jokes, that will not impact the main Plot, if they wish, or they can impact the Plot with humour. Satirical lampooning of the Dominant force can act to reduce their control, etc.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Persona specifically via their Motivation. An Idol may be tempted by Social Interactions and a crowd of cheering grateful masses.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The set up is the beginning of the joke, in a comedy this is usually a misunderstanding, in a shorter joke this is a question, somewhere in between it is a sentence or two that creates a narrative framework that misleads the audience. At its very simplest it is a cliché, such as a bunch of adventurers walk into a bar. Occasionally, it is a physical set up to a prank or stunt that will be important later. And every now and then it is a literal set up, with a situation created to put the PCs in the Narrative.
Yarn.Gain: Friends (a Group of 2-12 Vexes, or 2-6 Chorus Extras)
Yarn.Fray_Description: Capers are usually Low to Medium Stakes (although Higher Stakes Capers are possible) where multiple deceptions, confusions and complex physical activities occur that are all connected to trying to achieve some goal, often a heist or con-game. Humour is usually part of a Caper, such as swapping priceless jewels for plastic kids jewellery, or million-dollar bonds for monopoly money. In a real criminal enterprise such frivolities would create additional leads for the police, during a Caper though they will just confound, bemuse and amuse.
Yarn.Snag_Description: Someone, or occasionally something, is out to cause mischief. The Fray may be complicated by additional pranks, deceptions, antics and high-jinx. Then again, perhaps following the Fray there is some additional escapades of tom-foolery that the PCs must suffer through. This Snag can often be a sign that a capricious foe or Rival is involved in the Plot, such as those who carry Færy-blood.

NPC.Description: Clowns are a type of entertainer that act as though they are separate to society and its norms. The Clown's performance while comedic is based on the idea that the absurd is normal for the Character, whereas the normal is extraordinary. Not to be confused with the Fool.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: Comedy is an easy way to release Tension. A joke during a Tense moment can ruin a Thriller though, so use carefully. Comedy Relief can help Characters come to terms with recent events though. Often a Character may be comic relief, a Comic relief Character receives
Yarn.History_Description: The Joke indicates that the history of the current situation is funny somehow, there may be a joke, or just some humorous twists and situations.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Joke indicates the current situation is not a serious one, it may be a joke, a prank or simply a funny story. This is not usually a dangerous situation, although sometimes it might appear so at first. Sometimes the humour may begin to run amok and a Jape can become a farce, where anything that can go wrong will, at least humorously.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Joke indicates that the way out of the current situation is through humiliation, this may mean humiliating some one else (often a bully) or humiliating yourself.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about your own side, or own self. This might reveal your weaknesses or strengths, if you aren't already painfully aware of them.
Revelations.Info_Description: The information gained is in the form of a grotesque, this is information about how the Embodiment is viewed by others, but not simply a description. This is more a caricature, with the worst features emphasised usually for comedic effect. At their simplest grotesques can seem little more than a "Your Moma" joke, but a truly great grotesque will not only draw attention to some ugliness of spirit, but will draw some hybrid like comparison to an animal, that matches the Embodiment's temperament.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is disguised within a joke that someone tells. It might be the punchline, but is most likely to be a part of the set-up of the joke.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: This alternate information is about the humour that surrounds the Embodiment. If they are a character this may be their own preferred style of humour, perhaps even their favourite joke, occasionally (and for Descendants, Locations, etc) this humour will be about or feature the Embodiment, it is a joke told about them, or about the place, or that features them.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Fun details are those weird pieces of information that are often surprising and somewhat incongruous with other knowledge about them. This can lead to these details being known as "Fun Facts".

Obstacle.Description: Sometimes the way is blocked by some prank or facade, pranks are a type of psych snare, that can provide both positive and negative emotional effects, usually negative to the target but with positive effects on an audience. A bucket of water over a door, or a lubed banana-skin under a rug are examples. You may draw again and create a humorous version of that Obstacle.

Tarot.Normal: Gain a hint from the Ref or Yarn-Teller.
Tarot.Crossed: +1 Failure Level to all Perception, Investigation and Tracking Actions.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: A small gathering of influential, or professional people, or a route over something.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: First to get their boat through the final Stage, wins.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing forces will be jeering and mocking the Ebbing enemy for what is happening, maybe they lost their sword, perhaps they just charged into a pit trap, or maybe perhaps their trousers just fell off while they were running. The Flowing side has a +1 Success Level to any social or Psyche attacks.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A solitary Monster, at least comparable to a PC, that has at least two ♥ Monster Facets.
  Facet    Monster  
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
  Facet    Monster

Stress.Description: Stress can make Characters feel tense and a natural way to relieve that tension and Stress is by making jokes, even if they are inappropriate to the situation at hand.
Stress.Rules: Making a joke will relieve 1 Stress at any time, even if no one laughs because it is so inappropriate a joke or time.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can alter a Character’s sense of humour dramatically, things they previously found funny, may now seem cruel and malicious. Conversely, things the Character previously would have found, trite, sickening, morbid, or boring may now strike them as hilarious. Some Character’s find their sense of humour is destroyed by a trauma, where as others find it to be the ultimate coping mechanism. For others it is a change in styles of humour, moving from whatever they did find funny to puns and wordplay, or sarcasm, satire, prop-comedy, wit, shaggy-dog stories, offensive jokes, sick jokes, crude comedy, physical clowning, sight gags, punchlines, or dad jokes.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma alters the Character’s sense of humour in some way. The Relieved Stress from each Annex will change one thing the Character finds funny, either finding something new funny or not liking a style of humour they previously enjoyed. This is usually handled by the Character relieveing 1 point of Stress by laughing or groaning at a Joke that they previously would not or would have found funny, as appropriate.

Age.Description: An Age of Whimsy is occurring. During this age a society values play, sport, entertainment and escapism over industry or advancement. During an age of whimsy artists, musicians, sportsmen, actors, writers, and similar individuals are seen as social leaders and revolutionaries, and new artforms and styles will emerge during the age. Politics during this age is often more driven by character than by policy, which can lead to popularism and extremist politics arising to end the age.
Age.Rules: A period of cultural growth and diversity that creates new artforms, musical genres, styles and fashions. The Age will often end with some reactionary event or social force.
